blurb: Flat, blue-grey leaves held edge-on to the sun and rimmed in purple, angled like little propeller blades up an upright stem. The purple edging deepens the brighter you grow it.

care.light: Full sun to very bright light gives the strongest purple leaf margins and a compact, upright habit; in low light it stretches and greens out.
care.water: Soak-and-dry, letting the mix dry fully between waterings; markedly less in winter.
care.dormancy: A warm-season grower that rests in winter — keep it drier and brighter then.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice through spring and summer.
care.potting: Repot every year or two; cut back and re-root to keep it from getting leggy.
care.propagation: Stem cuttings root easily after a day or two to callus.
care.bloom: Small yellow-orange daisy-like flower heads on tall stalks.
care.troubles: Legginess in low light and rot from overwatering; aphids and mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Senecios are toxic to cats and dogs if eaten — best kept out of reach.
